zh.lifeSummary: 當Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel於1304年出生在德國海因里希_普洛文次 韓諾福格廷根時，他的父親Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen36歲，母親Rixa von Werle36歲。他於大約1327年與Sophia von Brandenburg結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1369年7月在他的家鄉享年65歲，埋葬在德國尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 盧包格盧包格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel于1304年出生在德国海因里希_普洛文次 韓諾福格廷根时，他的父亲Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen36岁，母亲Rixa von Werle36岁。他于大约1327年与Sophia von Brandenburg结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1369年7月在他的家乡享年65岁，埋葬在德国尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 盧包格盧包格。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Magnus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Latin byname meaning ‘great’, this was first extracted from the name of Charlemagne (recorded in Latin chronicles as Carolus Magnus ‘Charles the Great’) and used as a given name by the Scandinavians. It was borne by seven medieval kings of Norway, including Magnus I ( 1024–47 ), known as Magnus the Good, and Magnus VI ( 1238–80 ), known as Magnus the Law Mender. There are several early Scandinavian saints called Magnus, including an earl of Orkney ( d. 1116 ), to whom Kirkwall cathedral is dedicated. The name was imported to Scotland and Ireland during the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Mànas
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of   Magnus .

en.lifeSummary: When Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel was born in 1304, in Göttingen, Hanover, Prussia, Germany, his father, Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen, was 36 and his mother, Rixa von Werle, was 36. He married Sophia von Brandenburg about 1327. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died in July 1369, in his hometown, at the age of 65, and was buried in Lüneburg, Lüneburg, Lower Saxony, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel est né en 1304, à Göttingen, Hanovre, Prusse, Allemagne, son père, Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Rixa von Werle, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Sophia von Brandenburg environ 1327. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en juillet 1369, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Lüneburg, Landkreis Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel 1304 in Göttingen, Provinz Hannover, Preußen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen 36 und seine Mutter Rixa von Werle 36. Er hat um 1327 Sophia von Brandenburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb im Juli 1369 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Lüneburg, Landkreis Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel è nato il nel 1304 nel Göttingen, Provinz Hannover, Preußen, Germania, il suo padre, Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Rixa von Werle, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Sophia von Brandenburg circa 1327. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel luglio 1369, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Lüneburg, Landkreis Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttelが1304年にドイツプレウssンプロヴィンズ ハノバーグoッティンジェンで生まれた時，父Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhenは36歳，母Rixa von Werleは36歳であった。約1327年にSophia von Brandenburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1369年7月に故郷で65歳で死去し，ドイツニーダーサチセンランドクレイス ルuネブルグルuネブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel(이)가 1304년 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 해노버 그o틴겐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Rixa von Werle(은)는 36세였다. 그는 대략 1327년 Sophia von Brandenburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1369년 7월 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 니더작센 랜드크레이스 u니버그 u니버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel nasceu em 1304, em Göttingen, Provinz Hannover, Preußen, Alemanha, seu pai, Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Rixa von Werle, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Sophia von Brandenburg aproximadamente 1327. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em julho de 1369, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Lüneburg, Landkreis Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel родился в 1304 г. в местности гoттинген, провинз ханновир, Пруссия, Германия, его отцу по имени Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Rixa von Werle, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Sophia von Brandenburg около 1327 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер июль 1369 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности лuнебург, ландкрис лuнебург, ниедирсачсен, Германия.

sm.Bet. 1345–1369: mai le 1345 e o'o I le 1369

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Magnus I. Herzog von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel nació en 1304, en Göttingen, Provinz Hannover, Preußen, Alemania, su papá, Albrecht II. der Fette von Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel-Göttinhen, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Rixa von Werle, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Sophia von Brandenburg aproximadamente 1327. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en julio de 1369, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Lüneburg, Landkreis Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Alemania.
